title: Freedom Score
subtitle: Data by [Freedom House](#dod:freedom-house). The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy. Higher scores indicate more freedom.
caption:
Freedom Score

Data by Freedom House. The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy. Higher scores indicate more freedom.

The 2025 world map displays Freedom Scores using a color gradient where pale yellow represents the lowest scores (near 20) and dark blue represents the highest scores (up to 101). North America, Western Europe, Australia, and Japan are shaded in the darkest blue, indicating scores in the highest range. Most of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, along with India and South Africa, appear in medium blue tones, corresponding to the middle-to-upper segments of the scale. Large portions of Africa and the Middle East are colored in pale yellow and light green, signifying scores generally below 60. Russia and China are depicted in pale yellow, placing them in the lower score bands. Greenland and several smaller island nations are marked with diagonal hatching to denote no data. The visual distribution shows a concentration of high-scoring nations in the Northern Hemisphere's western and developed regions, while lower scores are prevalent across the African continent and parts of Asia. The legend at the bottom defines the scale from 20 to 101, with distinct color breaks at 40, 60, and 80 separating the five visible data categories.
